source_name: CDC ACIP DTaP and Tdap page; BOOSTRIX; ADACEL

source_name: UKHSA hexavalent programme guidance; EMA Infanrix Hexa; Merck VAXELIS PI

source_name: Health New Zealand immunisation history; Medsafe INFANRIX HEXA datasheet

source_name: Health NZ school booster standards; Medsafe BOOSTRIX datasheet

source_name: Health New Zealand immunisation history; Health NZ immunisation of special groups; Medsafe BOOSTRIX datasheet
notes: Boostrix has been recommended in every pregnancy since July 2015.

source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
notes: Acellular pertussis vaccine was used first for booster doses in 1997-1998 before all-dose routine replacement.

product_name: Australian routine acellular childhood programme summary
source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter
notes: Post-1999 Australian routine acellular products contain PRN.

product_name: Australian routine acellular booster programme summary
source_name: Australian Immunisation Handbook pertussis chapter; NCIRS pertussis factsheet
notes: Current Australian booster products curated here are PRN-positive.

source_name: Finnish epidemiology study documenting the 2005 replacement of whole-cell vaccine by acellular vaccine; Finnish antigen-expression study describing PT/FHA-only followed by PT/FHA/PRN acellular vaccine use
notes: The first Finnish acellular interval used PT/FHA-only products without PRN.

source_name: Finnish antigen-expression study describing PT/FHA-only followed by PT/FHA/PRN acellular vaccine use
notes: Finnish routine products reintroduced PRN from 2009.

source_name: RIVM Vaxelis transition report; RIVM product information Vaxelis; Dutch medicines database Vaxelis
notes: RIVM materials support the switch from Infanrix hexa to Vaxelis for the 2019 birth cohort onward; the Dutch medicines database lists PRN in Vaxelis, so current primary-series exposure is curated as PRN-positive.

source_name: RIVM 2025 schedule change; ReportLinker pediatric pertussis combination vaccines market study; Dutch medicines database Boostrix Polio
notes: RIVM states that the 5-year booster previously also included polio as DTaP-IPV, and ReportLinker lists Boostrix-IPV for Netherlands school-entry vaccination in 2020; the Dutch medicines database lists PRN in Boostrix Polio, so pre-2025 booster exposure is PRN-positive.

source_name: RIVM product information Boostrix; Dutch medicines database Boostrix; RIVM 2025 schedule change
notes: RIVM product information identifies Boostrix for the DKT booster vaccination of 5-year-olds after the 2025 schedule change; the Dutch medicines database lists PRN in Boostrix, so current booster exposure is PRN-positive.

source_name: RIVM 22-week vaccination launch; RIVM product information Boostrix; Dutch medicines database Boostrix
notes: RIVM announced free 22-week maternal pertussis vaccination from 16 December 2019; RIVM product information identifies Boostrix for pregnancy use and the Dutch medicines database lists PRN in Boostrix.

source_name: Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; CDC review of pertactin-deficient expansion
notes: Japan-specific literature reports that three of five long-used DTaP brands contained PRN before the 2012 DTaP-IPV switch; represented as a 60% historical brand-count proxy.

source_name: Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; CDC review of pertactin-deficient expansion
notes: Japan-specific literature reports that two of five long-used DTaP brands lacked PRN before the 2012 DTaP-IPV switch; represented as a 40% historical brand-count proxy.

product_name: DTaP-IPV brands before PRN-containing 2015 product introduction
source_name: Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study; National Institute of Public Health Japan report on pertussis-containing vaccine antigen composition
notes: Japan-specific literature reports all four DTaP-IPV brands used after November 2012 lacked PRN before an additional PRN-containing DTaP-IPV brand was introduced in December 2015.

product_name: Current pediatric DTaP summary (TRIBIK/GOBIK-class; unresolved PRN-containing brand share)
source_name: National Institute of Public Health Japan report on pertussis-containing vaccine antigen composition; PMDA TRIBIK product information; PMDA GOBIK product information; Japanese PRN-deficient surveillance study
notes: NIPH 2025 supports the current pediatric DTaP reference product as PT/FHA-only, but the EID study reports an additional PRN-containing DTaP-IPV brand introduced in December 2015; exact post-2015 market share remains unresolved, so this row is PRN-negative dominant/current rather than a fully resolved product-share estimate.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: Spain-specific study defines 1998-2005 as the transition from WCV to ACV; exact annual product shares are not resolved, so this row is represented as a 50/50 transition proxy.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: Spain introduced acellular booster vaccination in 1998 during the WCV-to-ACV transition interval.

source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: The Spain-specific study defines 2006 onward as the ACV-only period until the later 2013 regional PRN-free split.

product_name: Regional ACV programmes retaining PRN-containing products
source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: Spain-specific literature reports approval in 2013 of a vaccine without PRN for primary vaccination in 5 of 19 regions; the remaining 14 of 19 regions are represented as retaining PRN-containing acellular exposure.

product_name: Regional ACV programmes using PRN-free product after 2013 approval
source_name: Spanish pertactin-deficiency study
notes: Spain-specific literature reports approval in 2013 of a vaccine without PRN for primary vaccination in 5 of 19 regions; those 5 of 19 regions are represented as PRN-free acellular exposure.

source_name: Czech 2018 hexavaccine recommendation; EMA Infanrix hexa

source_name: Czech 2018 hexavaccine recommendation; EMA Hexacima
